verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Main blocker: wind is over 30 km/h.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY there’s no really reliable window, and with the wind looking too strong for comfort at Baleal it’s probably not worth chasing. Surfline is only poor to fair, so even though lunch could line up with a bit of push for the sandbanks, the wind is the main thing that’s likely to spoil it. I’d check Surfline and MEO Beachcam first before you make any plans—unless the cams look way cleaner than expected, I’d save the drive.

best.windFit: N 30 km/h · cross-onshore · too windy, strong
best.bestWind: ideal E/ESE/SE, usable NE/ENE/SSE, clean <=25 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Baleal looks like a fun-sized window for Safe fun, Medium confidence: 0.9-1.2 m, 258 kJ, 10 s period, consistent 68/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push; 10-13s at mid tide suits the Baleal sandbanks and gives workable peaks; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: wind is over 30 km/h; wind fit is poor or too strong.
best.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind fit is poor or too strong
